Posting Stats to Leaderboards

Your game can write stats to the stats service by calling either the XOnlineStatWrite or XOnlineStatWriteEx function. With XOnlineStatWrite, your game can write one or more stats for one or more players. The statistical values it writes can be different for every player. For example, the game could write ratings and rankings for some players while writing fastest times and highest scores for others.

XOnlineStatWrite writes stats to the stats service by sending an array of XONLINE_STAT_SPEC structures. Each XONLINE_STAT_SPEC structure contains a user ID and a leaderboard ID. In addition, it contains an array of XONLINE_STATstructures. Each item in the array of XONLINE_STATstructures hold the type and the value for one statistic. Your game is limited to a maximum of five per player XONLINE_STAT_SPEC structures per call to XOnlineStatWrite. It can call XOnlineStatWrite a maximum of once every 300 milliseconds. If it is called more often than that, the additional calls are ignored.

XOnlineStatWriteEx provides extended write capabilities to your title. It sends an array of XONLINE_STAT_PROC structures to the stats service. The XONLINE_STAT_PROC structure enables a wider variey of operations than the XONLINE_STAT_SPEC structure. For example, your game can do conditional updates to the stats on a leaderboard. This means, for instance, that your game can update the stats only if the new value is greater than the old value. In addition, XONLINE_STAT_PROC structures enable your game to increment existing stats, create ELO ratings, and customize stats.

The XOnlineStatWriteEx function can be called a maximum of once every 5 minutes. If it is called more often than that, the additional calls are ignored.

Unpublished work. © 2000 Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.
